channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korea and the US will begin annual military drills next week
item.description: South Korean and U.S. troops will begin their large annual joint military drills next week to enhance their readiness against North Korean threats.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China's premier and the American president: Two leaders, two speeches, two differing world visions
item.description: Two major speeches were delivered by two of the world’s most powerful world leaders this week, on opposite sides of the planet.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Zealand's most senior diplomat in London loses his job over remarks about President Trump
item.description: New Zealand’s High Commissioner to the United Kingdom has lost his job over remarks he made about President Donald Trump at an event held by the international affairs think tank Chatham House in London this week.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Majority say US, not other countries, will feel brunt of Trump tariffs: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans in a new poll said that the U.S. and not other countries will feel the brunt of President Trump’s tariffs. When asked about “the cost of tariffs on foreign products imported into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russian missile experts visited Iran amid growing military ties
item.description: Russian military specialists visited Iran in 2024 as the two countries strengthen their cooperation in the fields of missile technology according to recent reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU leaders to hold emergency Ukraine talks, seeking to adapt to new security demands without the US
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders plan to hold emergency talks on Thursday to agree ways to quickly increase their military budgets after the Trump administration signaled that Europe must take care of its own security and also suspended assistance to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: President Donald Trump has issued what he calls a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOJ charges Chinese hackers, government officials in campaign targeting US agencies
item.description: The U.S. government has charged 10 Chinese hackers-for-hire and two of the country's security officials in a scheme to steal data from American entities and dissidents of the Chinese government, the Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon denies reports Hegseth ordered halt in cyber operations against Russia
item.description: The Department of Defense is denying reports Secretary Pete Hegseth halted offensive cyber operations against Russia.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin says a 2022 Ukrainian decree bans Zelenskyy from talks with Putin
item.description: Russia on Wednesday asked how Ukraine could attend potential talks on ending their three-year war when a Ukrainian decree from 2022 rules out negotiations with President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Israel’s cutoff of supplies to Gaza sends prices soaring as aid stockpiles dwindle
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s cutoff of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies to Gaza’s 2 million people has sent prices soaring and humanitarian groups into overdrive trying to distribute dwindling stocks to the most vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: The US is holding direct talks with Hamas
item.description: White House press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that U.S. officials have had “ongoing talks and discussions” with Hamas officials, as the ceasefire in Gaza between Israel and Hamas hangs in the balance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Serbian lawmakers throw smoke grenades, tear gas in parliament
item.description: Serbian opposition lawmakers let off smoke grenades and tear gas in parliament on Tuesday to protest against the government and support demonstrating students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indigenous migrants in northern Colombia battle worsening droughts and floods
item.description: MAICAO, Colombia (AP) — Standing next to her makeshift home of scrap metal, wood and plastic tarp, 47-year-old Nelly Mengual recounts how severe flooding and winds tore off her roof a few months ago, leaving her knee-deep in water in her home.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Department of Education clarifies school DEI guidelines, saying Black History Month doesn't violate order
item.description: The Department of Education clarified that celebrating Black History Month is not in violation of an order warning states to remove DEI policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis to resume mechanical ventilation as his condition remains stable, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis will resume "non-invasive mechanical ventilation" Tuesday despite his condition remaining stable, the Vatican announced in an evening update.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU ponders 800 billion euro plan to beef up defenses to counter possible US disengagement
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The chief of the European Union's executive on Tuesday proposed an 800 billion-euro ($841 billion) plan to beef up the defenses of EU nations, aiming to lessen the impact of potential U.S.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s threats against Canada boost Trudeau's Liberal Party in the polls as new tariffs set to begin
item.description: Concern over Trump’s tariffs and annexation has found Canadians leaning Liberal with the prospect of a new leader who is set to take over from Trudeau in the next few days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on Greenlanders resisting Trump's talk of acquiring their homeland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — Greenlanders have been pushed into the global spotlight in the weeks since U.S. President Donald Trump said America could take over their Arctic homeland. Most say they don’t want to be American. Many are worried and overwhelmed by the comments and the attention.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis suffers 2 acute respiratory crises, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two episodes of acute respiratory distress Monday as his prognosis remains "guarded" while at a hospital in Rome, the Vatican announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire, while key mediator Egypt accused Israel of using “starvation as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House backs Israel's decision to halt Gaza aid shipments until Hamas accepts ceasefire extension
item.description: Israel's move to cut all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip has the support of the White House, according to a statement form the National Security Council.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel agrees to Trump envoy’s temporary Gaza ceasefire extension proposal as first phase expires: reports
item.description: As the first phase of the temporary ceasefire in Gaza ends, the Israeli government said Saturday night it had agreed to a proposal by Trump envoy Steve Witkoff to extend it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy meets with British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in London following Trump Oval Office clash
item.description: A day after his White House clash with President Trump, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was greeted in London by Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — When Israeli snipers took positions in her neighborhood, Haleemeh Zawaydeh knew her family needed to leave quickly. As the snipers' gunfire rang out, the 63-year-old matriarch said there was no time to pack as she and 14 other family members fled on foot.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, AREF TUFAHA and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Andrew Tate, who faces rape, trafficking charges in Romania, has left for US
item.description: The brothers are also charged with forming a criminal gang to sexually exploit women. Andrew Tate also faces an additional charge of rape.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and ANDREEA ALEXANDRU, Associated Press, NewsNation
